creator: jon@xyinn.org
text: Yup exactly! That's what I was trying to say. The ntpd wasn't working and even with the sync on start it didn't want to slew and fix itself. The only thing that worked (without using ntpdate) was to actually manually fix the clock in the BIOS. With FreeBSD being set to use the BIOS' localtime and the clock being properly set, it allowed ntpd to be within the proper range to allow updates ;).
